name: Johnny Green
abstract: John Waldo Green (October 10, 1908 – May 15, 1989) was an American songwriter, composer, musical arranger, conductor and pianist. He was given the nickname "Beulah" by colleague Conrad Salinger.

works_created.t: Raintree County: Three Themes for Symphony Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And the conductor, Johnny Green, he was very surprised and happy, because it really did help put things in context.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: They had Barney Bigard and Johnny Green and Joe Bushkin. It was a big affair.

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: some of them like Johnny Green they came from dance band experience.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I got to work with Johnny Green and Max Steiner and Alfred Newman...
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: He was the assistant musical director at MGM with Johnny Green.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012
